 {"id":"bd-pn6c.2","title":"Add test for on_exit handler invocation order","description":"Test that on_exit handlers are:
1. Called during exit() in reverse registration order (LIFO)
2. Receive the correct exit status and arg
3. Don't interfere with atexit handlers

NOTE: Testing exit() directly is tricky since it terminates the process. Options:
1. Test the internal ON_EXIT_HANDLERS directly in unit tests
2. Use fork() + waitpid to test exit behavior in a child process
3. Test just the handler registration and manual invocation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:14.048093413Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:41:54.598111282Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:41:54.597018413Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pn6c.2","depends_on_id":"bd-pn6c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:14.048093413Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-pn6c.3","title":"Investigate and fix the 3 pre-existing pow errno test failures","description":"Three tests have been failing throughout all audit rounds:
- pow_overflow_sets_range_errno
- pow_underflow_sets_range_errno
- pow_zero_negative_sets_range_errno

All expect errno == ERANGE after pow() calls, but errno remains 0. The pow() function correctly calls set_range_errno() which calls set_abi_errno(libc::ERANGE). The test reads errno via __errno_location().

HYPOTHESIS: In test mode, something between set_abi_errno and the test's abi_errno() read is clearing errno. Possible causes:
1. runtime_policy::observe() or decide() inadvertently calls something that sets errno
2. The thread-local errno in test mode is different from the one used by set_abi_errno
3. A Rust standard library operation between the pow call and the assertion resets errno

DEBUG APPROACH: Add eprintln! tracing to set_abi_errno and __errno_location in test mode to verify they use the same TLS slot.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:15.268801545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:27:11.456442127Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:27:11.455469646Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pn6c.3","depends_on_id":"bd-pn6c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:15.268801545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ppg9","title":"regex_glob_ops conformance: add wordexp differential fixtures","description":"Background:
- `wordexp` now has ABI host-parity coverage, but the conformance harness family for regex/glob operations still has no fixture-driven `wordexp` execution.
- `regex_glob_ops` currently validates regcomp/regexec/fnmatch/glob only.

Goal:
- Extend the regex/glob conformance harness with fixture-backed `wordexp` differential execution and coverage.

Deliverables:
1) Add `wordexp` cases to `tests/conformance/fixtures/regex_glob_ops.json`.
2) Teach `crates/frankenlibc_conformance` to execute `wordexp` fixtures against host glibc and frankenlibc ABI.
3) Upgrade the harness test so the regex/glob fixture family executes through `TestRunner` and asserts pass/host parity.

Verification:
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test regex_glob_ops_conformance_test -- --nocapture
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc_conformance regex_glob_ops_fixture_cases_match_execute_fixture_case -- --nocapture
- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:17:22.285947131Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:27:24.327945186Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:27:24.325950507Z","close_reason":"Added wordexp differential fixtures and regex_glob_ops conformance coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-pt6","title":"Perf: Profiling recipe (flamegraph/perf) for runtime_math hot path","description":"Define the mandatory profile-first loop for runtime_math.

Acceptance criteria:
- Document exact commands to produce CPU flamegraphs for decide/observe and pointer validation call sites.
- Identify top-5 hotspots and track shifts after changes.

Notes:
- This is the enforcement mechanism for extreme-software-optimization: no optimization or new kernel merges without re-profiling.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueLake","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:56.638632908Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T02:23:50.824940609Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T02:23:50.824922124Z","close_reason":"profiling recipe documented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1133,"issue_id":"bd-pt6","author":"BlueLake","text":"Implemented profiling recipe doc at scripts/PROFILING_RUNTIME_MATH.md.

Includes:
- Exact cargo flamegraph/perf commands for runtime_math decide/observe/decide_observe and pointer validation validate_known.
- Perf permissions + debuginfo guidance.
- Top-5 hotspot extraction command and example top-5 lists (captured 2026-02-10 strict in this workspace).","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:22:51Z"}]}
